name: Jesse Stone
description: American rhythm and blues musician and songwriter (1901-1999)
abstract: Jesse Albert Stone (November 16, 1901 – April 1, 1999) was an American rhythm and blues musician and songwriter whose influence spanned a wide range of genres. He also used the pseudonyms Charles Calhoun and Chuck Calhoun.

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Perhaps you know the name Jesse Stone. [INTERVIEWEE] Yes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: He was the leader of the band... I'm rehearsing with Jesse Stone every day... That's when I signed up to go on the USO.

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: we were all together in this Jesse Stone band from the North Dallas Club.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Durham
about_quotes.evidence: That's when we all went into Jesse Stone's band, the same way.

about_quotes.speaker: Vic Dickenson
about_quotes.evidence: We had a swell arranger in that band, too, named Jesse Stone.

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: he told me, he said, 'Well, Panama, everything is gonna be all right' -- and so he was recording, he started recording with Atlantic Records with Jesse Stone and Leroy Kirkland.
